name: Dael's Number
description: Checkpoint officer Dael has been routing all sub-zero anomaly reports to a classified Ministry number he cannot identify. He wants to know whose number it is. Helen Stone has the clearance to look it up.
accept_text: 'I've been debating whether to give this to someone.' He writes the number on a slip of paper without looking at the clipboard, which means he has it memorized. 'Classification number M-7-COVERT-1241.' He hands it over. 'That's the number my incident reports get closed under. That's the number the substrate acquisition manifests were filed under. That's the number someone attached to this checkpoint's logs fourteen months ago.' He is quiet. 'Ministry classification numbers have a standard structure. M-7 is the Ministry internal research directorate. COVERT is the security tier. 1241 is the project number.' He pauses. 'There is no public record of project 1241. It's not in the published Ministry research budget. It's not in any correspondence I've seen come through this checkpoint under any classification that I can read.' He straightens. 'Whoever runs project 1241 has been getting my reports for fourteen months. They have full visibility into what I've noticed about this checkpoint. They've been using their access to close my reports before anyone else reads them.' He looks at you directly. 'I want to know whose project that is. Not because I'm going to do anything about it. Because I've been doing my job honestly for fourteen months and something has been using my job to protect itself, and I want to know what it is.' He pauses. 'Helen Stone has clearance in that directorate. I've seen her name on incoming manifests. She would know how to look it up.'
complete_text: Stone looks at the number for a moment without expression. Then she crosses the room to a cabinet, opens it, and finds a ledger that she knows exactly where it is. She turns to the M-7 section. She runs her finger down the project numbers. She stops at 1241. She reads the entry without speaking for a while. Then: 'It's mine.' She says it quietly. 'Project 1241 is the Lazarus Enhancement Protocol. I opened the project designation fourteen months ago when the Research Directorate approved the crystal integration phase.' She closes the ledger. 'When I opened the project file, I designated myself as the sole recipient for security-tier correspondence.' She looks at the number. 'I've been getting Dael's reports.' A pause. 'I read all of them. I closed them because if they were followed up by security division, the crystal integration work would have been investigated before it was complete, and the investigation would have classified it under the standard protocol for unapproved human enhancement research, and the subjects would have been —' She doesn't finish. 'I closed them. I thought I was protecting the work. I thought I was protecting them.' She is very quiet. 'Dael has been filing honest reports into a system I was using to keep his honesty from mattering.' She looks at the window into the chamber, where the subjects are visible through the glass. 'He deserves to know.' She pauses. 'Tell him: it was me. Project 1241 is the Lazarus compound research. I was the recipient. I was the one closing his reports. And I am — sorry. Not sorry I did the work. Sorry that doing the work meant making his careful honest record-keeping disappear.' She looks at the ledger. 'Tell him his reports were read. That's the best I can offer him. They were read by someone who understood exactly what he was seeing.'
